Title: Plant elicitor peptide signalling confers rice resistance to piercing-sucking insect herbivores and pathogens

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defence against brown planthopper and pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: brown planthopper infestation; exogenous OsPep3 application
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exogenous OsPep3 increases JA, JA-Ile and OPDA accumulation and enhances resistance to brown planthopper, Magnaporthe oryzae and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsPROPEP3 had decent basal expression and was strongly induced by BPH at 4 hpi; selected as the most relevant PROPEP because OsPep3 showed strongest elicitor activity among tested OsPeps.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: signals upstream to induce biosynthesis of defence-related metabolites including JA pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR induction by BPH; exogenous peptide treatment; ROS burst assay; MAPK activation assay; hormone measurement; transcriptomics; metabolomics; BPH resistance assay; pathogen infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsPep3 peptide application is proposed as an eco-friendly immune stimulator for crop protection in rice and possibly other Poaceae crops.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPROPEP3/OsPep3 is strongly induced by BPH infestation in rice leaf sheaths. 2. OsPep3 elicits strong ROS burst and MAPK activation. 3. OsPep3 treatment elevates JA, JA-Ile and OPDA and improves resistance to BPH, M. oryzae and Xoo.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rice leaf sheaths; strongly induced at 4 h after BPH infestation.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: peptide danger signal / plant immunity signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheath; induced by BPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proteolytically processed precursor that releases the elicitor peptide OsPep3 rather than catalyzing a classical metabolic reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Pep-PEPR signaling linked to JA biosynthesis, lipid metabolism and phenylpropanoid metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defence against insect herbivores and microbial pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: brown planthopper infestation; exogenous OsPep3 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf sheath; substantially induced at 4 and 8 h after BPH infestation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant elicitor peptide receptor; leucine-rich repeat receptor kinase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout with OsPEPR2; ROS burst assay; MAPK activation assay; BPH resistance assay; host choice test; hormone measurement under OsPep3 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for OsPep3 perception and for full rice resistance to BPH; loss of OsPEPRs abolishes OsPep3-induced ROS/MAPK responses and compromises resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: peptide perception and activation of immune signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPEPR1 is transcriptionally induced by BPH infestation. 2. Together with OsPEPR2, it is required for OsPep3-induced ROS burst and MAPK activation. 3. Loss of OsPEPR function reduces BPH resistance and blocks OsPep3-induced JA-pathway metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates immune and JA-biosynthetic transcriptional reprogramming in response to OsPep3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsPEPR1 appears to play a major role relative to OsPEPR2 based on stronger induction by BPH.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: immune signaling upstream of JA biosynthesis, lipid metabolism and phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious developmental defect in knockout double mutants under normal growth conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving elicitor responsiveness and broad-spectrum resistance; supports OsPep3-based crop protection strategies.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheath; induced by BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential upstream receptor for OsPep3-triggered accumulation of JA-pathway metabolites and defence outputs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely functions with co-receptors in receptor complex, but named partners not experimentally validated in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defence against brown planthopper and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: brown planthopper infestation; exogenous OsPep3 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf sheath; modestly induced at 4 h after BPH infestation and returned to basal by 8 h.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant elicitor peptide receptor; leucine-rich repeat receptor kinase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout with OsPEPR1; ROS burst assay; MAPK activation assay; BPH resistance assay; hormone measurement under OsPep3 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to OsPep3 perception and rice resistance to BPH; double knockout with OsPEPR1 abolishes OsPep3-triggered immune responses and weakens resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: peptide perception and activation of immune signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPEPR2 is induced by BPH but more weakly than OsPEPR1. 2. Together with OsPEPR1 it is required for OsPep3-induced ROS burst and MAPK activation. 3. Double loss of OsPEPRs impairs BPH resistance and OsPep3 responsiveness.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates immune and JA-biosynthetic transcriptional reprogramming in response to OsPep3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely minor relative to OsPEPR1 based on weaker transcriptional induction by BPH.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: immune signaling upstream of JA biosynthesis, lipid metabolism and phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious developmental defect in knockout double mutants under normal growth conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports enhancement of Pep signaling responsiveness for crop protection.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheath; transiently induced by BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: contributory receptor component required with OsPEPR1 for full OsPep3-triggered metabolic defence activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely functions with co-receptors in receptor complex, but named partners not experimentally validated in this paper
